'''Frankie Mariano''' (born 10 May 1987) is a retired [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Featherstone Rovers]] (two spells, including the first on [[Loan (sports)|loan]]), the [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]) in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Betfred Championship]]

==Background==
Mariano was born in England and is of Scottish, [[American ancestry|American]] and [[Cape Verde]]an descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/all-clubs/mariano-is-gunning-for-glory-as-trinity-eye-play-offs-1-4905572|title=Mariano is gunning for glory as Trinity eye play-offs|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_14032-mariano-eyes-possible-scotland-future.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140329222456/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_14032-mariano-eyes-possible-scotland-future.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=29 March 2014|title=Mariano eyes possible Scotland future - Love Rugby League|date=16 January 2014}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Mariano made his first team début for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2009 in the [[Challenge Cup]] round 5 victory over the [[Sheffield Eagles]] which Hull Kingston Rovers won 34–24.

He moved to [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2011 and spent three years at the club, before joining the [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2014.

On 16 May 2014, Frankie's excellent form to start the season saw him sign a two-year deal to stay with the Tigers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/30225/mariano-signs-new-tigers-deal|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517121807/http://superleague.co.uk/article/30225/mariano-signs-new-tigers-deal|url-status=dead|archive-date=2014-05-17|title=MARIANO SIGNS NEW TIGERS DEAL|website=superleague.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/9314081/super-league-frankie-mariano-extends-contract-with-castleford-tigers|title=Super League: Frankie Mariano extends contract with Castleford Tigers|publisher=skysports.com}}</ref>

He featured for the Castleford Tigers in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

He featured in 2016 for the Castleford Tigers in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article7007081.ece|title=Leeds likely to finish top of the pile again|date=29 January 2010|work= [[The Times]]|access-date=11 March 2010}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> He previously played for Hull Kingston Rovers and the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats.
